suggestions.title: test_host_json_is_valid_json raises exception instead of asserting cleanly on invalid JSON
suggestions.description: json.load() will throw json.JSONDecodeError if the file contains malformed JSON. Pytest reports this as an ERROR (unexpected exception), not a clean FAIL, which can obscure CI output.
suggestions.recommendation: Wrap the parse in a try/except and re-assert with a human-readable message using pytest.fail().

suggestions.title: Every test independently opens and parses host.json — no shared fixture
suggestions.description: Six of seven tests each call HOST_JSON_PATH.open() and json.load() independently. If load logic needs to change, it must be updated in six places.
suggestions.recommendation: Introduce a module-scoped fixture and share it across tests.

suggestions.title: Downstream tests produce FileNotFoundError instead of a clean skip if host.json is absent
suggestions.description: If host.json were missing, test_host_json_exists would FAIL correctly, but the remaining six tests would each throw FileNotFoundError (ERROR, not FAIL), cluttering CI output.
suggestions.recommendation: Add a module-level guard in the shared fixture so downstream tests skip rather than error when the file is absent.

suggestions.title: No $schema declaration in host.json
suggestions.description: The Azure Functions host.json schema supports a $schema property for IDE tooling and documentation completeness.
